| `sum-range-mul-two-pow` вАФ For every natural n, the sum over i in 0..n-1 of i * 2^i equals (n - 2) * 2^n + 2 (stated over вД§, where the right side is negative-free for all n). | proved | 2 | вАФ | classic identities | Arithmetico-geometric sum вИСk¬Ј2^k = (n-2)¬Ј2^n + 2; Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, ¬І2.5 (the perturbation-method worked example вИСk¬Ј2^k). |
| `sum-range-odd-eq-sq` вАФ For every natural n, the sum over i in 0..n-1 of (2i+1) equals n^2 (i.e. 1+3+5+...+(2n-1) = n^2). | proved | 1 | [packet-ready](upstream/sum-range-odd-eq-sq.md) | classic identities | Classical 'gnomon' identity (sum of consecutive odd numbers is a perfect square). | `sum-range-pentagonal-closed-form` вАФ Closed form for the sum of the first n pentagonal numbers: $2 \sum_{k=0}^n \frac{3k^2-k}{2} = n^2(n+1)$. | proved | 3 | вАФ | classic figural number identities | Pentagonal number theorem for partial sums of pentagonal numbers. |
| `sum-range-pow-five-add-pow-seven` вАФ For every natural n, (sum of iвБµ for i in 0..n) + (sum of iвБЈ for i in 0..n) = 2¬Ј(sum of i for i in 0..n)вБі; i.e. вИСkвБµ + вИСkвБЈ = 2(вИСk)вБі = 2TвБі where T = n(n+1)/2 is the n-th triangular number. | proved | 4 | вАФ | classic identities (power-sum tower вАФ the **crown**: compounds on `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form` + `sum-range-pow-seven-closed-form`) | A classic Faulhaber curiosity: the sum of the fifth- and seventh-power sums is exactly twice the fourth power of the triangular number, generalising Nicomachus's вИСk¬≥ = (вИСk)¬≤ = T¬≤ one octave up. Verified вИА n; see Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993) for the triangular-number structure of odd-power sums. |
| `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form` вАФ For every natural n, 12 * (sum of i^5 for i in 0..n) = n^2 (n+1)^2 (2n^2 + 2n - 1); Faulhaber's closed form for fifth powers, вИСk^5 = (2n^6 + 6n^5 + 5n^4 - n^2)/12. | proved | 3 | вАФ | classic identities | Faulhaber's formula, p = 5 case; Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers (sums of powers); D. E. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables. |
| `sum-range-pow-five-faulhaber-triangular` вАФ For every natural n, 3¬Ј(sum of iвБµ for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)¬≤¬Ј(4¬Ј(sum of i for i in 0..n)вИТ1); i.e. вИСkвБµ = T¬≤(4TвИТ1)/3 where T = вИСk. Faulhaber's theorem made concrete: the fifth-power sum is a pure polynomial in the triangular number T. | proved | 3 | вАФ | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вАФ odd-power rung; compounds on `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form`) | Faulhaber's 1631 result that odd-power sums are polynomials in T = n(n+1)/2: вИСkвБµ = (4T¬≥вИТT¬≤)/3 = T¬≤(4TвИТ1)/3.
